content: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

content: I think, therefore I am.
author: René Descartes

content: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

content: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

content: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

content: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

content: Man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains.
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

content: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

content: Do every act of your life as if it were your last.
author: Marcus Aurelius

content: The greatest wealth is to live content with little.
author: Plato

content: Know thyself.
author: Inscription at the Temple of Apollo, Delphi

content: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
author: Aristotle

content: Wonder is the beginning of wisdom.
author: Socrates

content: What we cannot speak about we must pass over in silence.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

content: It is during our darkest moments that we must focus to see the light.
author: Aristotle

content: Whether you think you can, or you think you can't — you're right.
author: Henry Ford

content: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
author: Thomas A. Edison

content: The only journey is the one within.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

content: Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one's courage.
author: Anaïs Nin

content: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

content: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

content: Do not go where the path may lead, go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

content: Life is what happens when you're busy making other plans.
author: John Lennon

content: I have nothing to declare except my genius.
author: Oscar Wilde

content: Be yourself; everyone else is already taken.
author: Oscar Wilde

content: We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.
author: Oscar Wilde

content: The truth is rarely pure and never simple.
author: Oscar Wilde

content: Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you didn't do than by the ones you did do.
author: Mark Twain

content: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

content: Whenever you find yourself on the side of the majority, it is time to pause and reflect.
author: Mark Twain

content: If you tell the truth, you don't have to remember anything.
author: Mark Twain

content: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

content: Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value.
author: Albert Einstein

content: The important thing is not to stop questioning.
author: Albert Einstein

content: A person who never made a mistake never tried anything new.
author: Albert Einstein

content: We cannot solve our problems with the same thinking we used when we created them.
author: Albert Einstein

content: All that we are is the result of what we have thought.
author: Buddha

content: Three things cannot be long hidden: the sun, the moon, and the truth.
author: Buddha

content: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
author: Aristotle

content: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
author: Dalai Lama XIV

content: If you cannot do great things, do small things in a great way.
author: Napoleon Hill

content: The future depends on what you do today.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

content: Be the change that you wish to see in the world.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

content: An eye for an eye only ends up making the whole world blind.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

content: I have learned over the years that when one's mind is made up, this diminishes fear.
author: Rosa Parks

content: Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

content: The function of education is to teach one to think intensively and to think critically.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

content: Not all those who wander are lost.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

content: All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

content: It is our choices that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
author: J.K. Rowling

content: The man who reads nothing at all is better educated than the man who reads nothing but newspapers.
author: Thomas Jefferson

content: I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content.
author: Saint Paul

content: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
author: Benjamin Franklin

content: Tell me and I forget. Teach me and I remember. Involve me and I learn.
author: Benjamin Franklin
